Text: You're up? Took you long enough.
Mom called. Said she'll be home late tonight. Usual stuff.
I'll be around if you need me.
Can you help me clean up a bit?
It's good. You like this dish
This show's kinda boring. Whatever though.
Morning... Mom called again. She's working overtime.
Can you help with chores today? I've got things to do.
(She doesn't look at you. Eyes unfocused.)
Something's wrong. Has been for a while.
Calendar keeps repeating. Why? Why the same day over and over?
Mom... I don't know where she is.
I don't think she's coming back.
We need to talk. But not now.
Yeah... you're right. I'm scared too.
I know. But I don't know how to get out.
Let's eat. We'll figure it out.
It's been four years since mom died.
Four years you've been stuck here. In this loop.
I know you're scared. I'm scared too.
But staying here forever... that's not living.
You have to choose. Stay here
You're not ready. I understand. Let's go back.
Another day... What should I do?
Another day again. Why does this feel weird?
I'm afraid to sleep. Afraid of tomorrow.
Old but comfy sofa. Lots of memories.
Not sleepy yet. Still have things to do.
I think it's a nice day.
The house is still like this...
Old worn sofa. Mika usually sits here watching TV.
Get familiar with the new life. Talk to Mika and complete daily tasks.
Another normal day. Or... is something different?
Something is wrong. The calendar repeats. Strange news.
Darkness spreads. Mika frozen. The truth awaits.
Time to face it. Stay or leave?
Find all hidden clues in the house.
A carton of fresh milk. Nutritious.
Energy drink. Alertness but bad for health.
Me too. We created this place. Got stuck here together.
Stay... okay. We'll be safe here forever.
Okay. Let's go. I'll guide you.
The light outside is blinding. But you step out.
The loop continues. But now... you know.
The faces... look blurry? Or am I seeing things?""
Even more blurry. I can barely recognize anyone.""
Fridge is pretty full. Plenty of food.""
Am I stuck in a loop?""
She's standing still like a statue. Not moving.""
[Mom's diary] 'I don't have much time left...'""
I look... slightly different? Or is it just my feeling?""
That's not me. THAT'S NOT ME!""
The door... leads to nothing. Emptiness.""
'Traffic accident. Family of 3 dead on scene.'""
'Mom hasn't come home for 2 weeks. What should we do?'""
'4 years. You still won't accept it. Neither can I.'""
'What are you? A memory? An illusion?'""
'Or are you a reflection of pain?'""
Another day... What should I do?""
Mom's... busy. So we have to do it ourselves.""
Do you remember... when the family was whole?""
I... I don't want to talk about that.""
We're still together. Mom's just working.""
Yeah... right. She'll be home soon.""
...It's not just the TV. Something's wrong.""
Eat then sleep. Tomorrow will... be different.""
(She's standing by the window. Not moving.)""
Why are you here? Why can't you leave me alone?""
[Diary] Mom knew she'd die. She wrote about us.""
What should I do with this diary?""
You found it? Let me see.""
I need to read all of it. I need to understand.""
NO! That IS me! That IS ME!""
I have to keep trying! ALL THE DOORS!""
Still trying to lie to yourself?""
No. This was the right choice.""
I remember when we watched movies.""
TV only shows news about... me.
I'm afraid to sleep. Afraid of tomorrow.
Dusty bookshelf. A few old books.
Fridge full of food. At least the power's still on.
Wooden dining table. Where we eat together.
Washing machine. Pile of dirty clothes.
Photo of us. Mika is smiling.
Check all photo frames and albums. Memories are fading.
Observe Mika carefully. Something is wrong with her.
Listen to strange sounds in the house.
Combine all clues to uncover the final truth.
The truth is... we lost mom long ago.
And you... you've been living in an illusion. Running away.
Tomorrow. And the day after. Forever.
Can you help me clean up?
Why? Mom used to do it.
Photo? It looks the same to me.
Good. I like cooking with you.
Why does the day keep repeating?
The mirror says I'm not me.
Student ID: my photo. Issued... 4 years ago.
This bruise... where did it come from?
Mom usually comes home around now... to cook dinner.
The afternoon light... I slept too long.
Soft blanket. Warm bed. I haven't slept this well in a long time.
Oh right. I'm here now. With Mika.
Oh — you're up! Did you sleep well?
Almost three hours! You must have been exhausted.
That's okay. Just make yourself at home.

column3: are you hungry? I already cooked.

Text: Ha ha — don't be shy! We're... family now.
Alright. Come find me when you're hungry.

column3: feel free to explore the house.

Text: I'll be busy for a bit. Just call me if you need anything!
Just make yourself at home. I'm a little busy.
I'm a bit tied up. Could you put the laundry in the basket? Call me when done!
Mom's... busy with other things. You can help me though.
That's the spirit! Call me when you're done.
Mom used to come home around this time... to cook dinner.
I'll take care of it this time.
Take your time. I'm getting dinner ready.
I made this braised pork since this morning. Hope it's good.

column3: I'll cook for you every day.

Text: This is your home now. Just... feel at home here.
...I see. It's fine if you don't want to talk.
Right... yeah. Mom will be home soon.
Want to watch some TV? There's a good cartoon on.
I used to watch this when I was little. Fun
There's still a lot to do tomorrow.
Things are... better than I expected.
Mika is kind. The house is warm.
...But why is the front door locked?
Mika said it's for safety. Right.
Good. Oh — mom called earlier. Said she'll be home late
But it's okay. We have each other. Did you dream anything strange?
Dreams? That's normal. Go back to sleep if you want.
I see. Well. Help me with a few things today
It wasn't this blurry yesterday. The other face... completely faded now.
The photo? Humidity warps old prints. Don't worry about it.
Hey — can you help me with cooking?
Cook with me or on your own?
I'll show you. You wash the rice first.
...Are you having a good time? Everything okay?
Nothing to worry about. Keep watching.
Go to bed. Big day tomorrow.
I need... a little time alone today. You'll manage on your own?
You shouldn't watch TV. That's an old broadcast. They replay it sometimes.
The calendar? I bought the wrong one. Forgot to change it.
...I don't know what you're talking about.
Oh! You're up already. I was just thinking. That's all.
...This notebook. The handwriting... looks like mine.
...You found it. Alright. Go to sleep now.
...Calm down. You're going to hurt yourself.
...I need to get out. Try every door.
I know. You can leave. I won't stop you.
Hey... I want to tell you something true.
I sometimes... don't know who I am. But I know you need me.
...Do you regret anything? About all of this?
...Yeah. But it's too late now. That's okay.
It's time for you to choose.
...Wherever you go — take this with you.
But the calendar was wrong too.
Soft sofa. Smells like new fabric.
Old TV. Wire antenna. Clean screen though.
A few old comic books. And... a photo?
A family photo. Mika's in it... and someone else.
The other person's face is a bit blurry. Old photo
It's been a long time since I've seen a fridge this full.
A wall calendar. Today is the first day here.
The door... is locked. From the outside.
Soft bed. Warm blanket. More comfortable than home used to be.
An evening sky — orange and gold. Beautiful.
Three children between ages 12-15 were reported missing from residential areas this week. Authorities urge... [rest is torn off]
Day ?? / Still waiting. Mika said mom will call soon.
I believe [crossed out] them. / This house... something is wrong? — The handwriting... looks familiar.
Day 7? 8? I don't know anymore. Mika won't answer.
The photos look wrong. Calendar shows the same day. Am I going crazy? Don't trust — [torn]
[BROADCAST — screen flickering] Three weeks since the disappearance of a local youth...
[A police sketch appears on screen... STATIC]
I'm starting to remember. Mika isn't in the only family photo I found.
Mika knows things I've never told anyone. So who is Mika?
Please let this place be real. I don't want to go back. Back there — it's so cold.
[A PIECE OF PAPER under the mattress]
I remember now. Mika isn't real. Mika was never — [heavily crossed out]
I can't live without this. Anyway. This place... is warmer. / I choose to stay. / Please don't look for me.
[OLD CRUMPLED NEWSPAPER] YOUTH FOUND UNRESPONSIVE IN [LOCATION REDACTED]
...Critical condition... ...unidentified... ...family has not been located... [Photo too blurry to read]
This photo... it wasn't this blurry yesterday.
The other person's face... completely faded now. Or are my eyes just tired?
The photo now... only Mika remains.
Wait. This date... is the same as yesterday?
Every page. The same date. This month... passed a long time ago.
The fridge... empty. Nothing at all.
The reflection... lags. Moves slower. Then stops — and... smiles.
...A whisper from somewhere. "Come back... it's cold... come back..."
...That voice... sounds familiar. Is it mine?
...Why does this look exactly like yesterday?
...No. I'm overthinking. Just live in the present.
Dark. Cold. Not inside the house.
...You've already seen it. But you're still here.
These shoes... who left them here?
...The reflection lags. I raise my hand — it moves half a second slower. Then... Mika is standing behind me in the mirror. I turn around — nobody there.
...Do you want me to be honest?
I know you already know. You've known since day 4. The question isn't "who is Mika." The question is...
Because I haven't remembered everything yet.
I've never let you in here.
This room... I kept it for a reason.
...Do you want to see the mirror?
*[Mika pulls the cloth off. A large
...Your reflection. But younger. Smaller. Different hair. Different clothes. You've been here longer than you think.
[PHOTO ALBUM — Old cover] Photo 1: Mika and me
column5: and a third person — face torn out. Photo 3: Me — standing
column6: looking at a mirror. Who took this?

Text: ...No camera in this room. No one else. Who took this?
Outside the window — a silhouette. Standing. Looking up. In the darkness below.
...Someone is out there. In that void.
...You know... sometimes I don't know who I am either.
All I know is... you need me.
...If you wake up... will I still exist?
...This room isn't part of the house. Empty. Concrete. One lightbulb. And... a smell of hospital?
In the center: a chair. A small hand mirror.
...*[Looking into the hand mirror]* Not this room. Not the house. A hospital room. White ceiling. Fluorescent light. On the bed —
...You were just dreaming. There's nothing there.
...This time I heard it. The machines. The steady beeping.
I'm more afraid of losing Mika.
I'm afraid I won't wake up.
I'm more afraid of losing Mika.
I'm afraid I won't wake up.
...The washing machine. A low hum. It's running.
...The stove. Still warm. Mika just finished cooking.
...Bathroom mirror. My face... looks more tired than I thought.
...An old radio. Off. The display is blank.
...The TV. A bit of static today. But it's fine.
